hypothesis: When one modality is systematically corrupted or absent during fine-tuning (e.g., XRD patterns unavailable for amorphous materials), the quality of X-MoPA's cross-modal latent predictions for the missing modality—quantified by the held-out L2 reconstruction error from the remaining two modalities—will serve as a calibrated uncertainty estimator for downstream property prediction error, such that materials with higher cross-modal prediction discrepancy will exhibit proportionally larger fine-tuning MAE, enabling modality-absence-aware confidence scoring without requiring additional uncertainty quantification modules.
